title: centaurea maculosa subsp. subalbida
intro: Centaurea maculosa subsp. subalbida, a subspecies of the spotted knapweed plant, is a flowering plant native to Europe and Western Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other compounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, due to its antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. In herbal medicine, Centaurea maculosa subsp. subalbida can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve to be used topically for skin issues and internally for digestive problems.
